unit.name: ELBO derivation, reparameterization, and VAE latent-space behavior
unit.description: Derives the VAE training objective, shows how it is optimized in practice, and interprets the resulting latent space.
unit.summary: The lecture derives the VAE objective by starting from log p(x), rewriting it with Bayes' rule, introducing q(z|x), and decomposing the result into an expected reconstruction term plus two KL divergences [ts=3794s] [ts=3890s] [ts=3936s]. The first interpretable term says that samples drawn from the encoder's q(z|x) should reconstruct x through the decoder, and the second pushes the encoder's latent distribution toward the chosen prior p(z) [ts=3957s] [ts=4003s]. The last KL term, KL(q(z|x) || p(z|x)), is intractable because p(z|x) was the quantity we could not compute in the first place, so it is dropped using the fact that KL divergence is always nonnegative; this yields a lower bound on log p(x), the ELBO, which the VAE maximizes instead of exact likelihood [ts=4051s] [ts=4086s] [ts=4147s]. Training then consists of encoding x into a Gaussian over z, applying the KL-to-prior penalty, sampling z with the reparameterization trick so gradients can flow, and decoding back to a reconstruction term [ts=4164s] [ts=4193s] [ts=4208s]. The segment closes by explaining the tension between reconstruction and prior losses—one wants distinct near-deterministic codes, the other wants codes close to a unit Gaussian—and connects that tension to practical sampling and to the common observation that varying latent dimensions can smoothly morph generated digits in interpretable ways [ts=4226s] [ts=4282s] [ts=4310s].

key_points.text: The VAE objective decomposes into an expected reconstruction term and KL-divergence terms after rewriting log p(x) with Bayes' rule and q(z|x).
The reconstruction term encourages samples from q(z|x) to decode back to the original x.
The prior term measures how closely the encoder's latent distribution matches the chosen prior p(z).
Dropping the intractable KL(q(z|x) || p(z|x)) term because KL is nonnegative yields the ELBO lower bound.
The reparameterization trick allows sampling z from the encoder's Gaussian while still backpropagating through the sampling step.
In a trained VAE, the reconstruction and prior losses fight to balance faithful reconstruction with a sampleable latent space, often producing smooth latent interpolations.
